amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Antibiotic efflux pumps represent a critical defense mechanism employed by *Escherichia coli* against a broad spectrum of antimicrobial agents, significantly contributing to intrinsic and acquired antibiotic resistance. These sophisticated bacterial systems actively extrude antibiotics from the cell, thereby reducing their intracellular concentration below therapeutic levels. Key efflux pump families in *E. coli* include Resistance-Nodulation-Division (RND), Major Facilitator Superfamily (MFS), Small Multidrug Resistance (SMR), and Multidrug and Toxic Compound Extrusion (MATE). The most well-characterized RND pump, AcrAB-TolC, is a tripartite complex spanning both inner and outer membranes, capable of expelling diverse substrates such as fluoroquinolones and tigecycline.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A["Start: Bacterial Exposure to Antibiotics"] --> B[Antibiotic Molecules Enter Periplasm]
    A --> D[Inducers Present (e.g., Salicylate, Tetracycline, Bile Salts)]
    A --> E[Genetic Acquisition (e.g., Plasmid-encoded efflux genes)]
    A --> F[Increased Oxidative Stress Signal]

    B --> C[Antibiotic Molecules Enter Cytoplasm]
    C --> AT[Antibiotic Substrate Binds to Efflux Pump]
    B --> AT

    D --> M[/MarR Repressor Inactivation/Degradation/]
    D --> O[Inducer Binding to Rob]
    F --> N[SoxR Activation (e.g., by redox change)]
    E --> V[Plasmid-encoded Efflux Gene Transcription]

    M --> P[MarA Transcriptional Activator]
    N --> Q[SoxS Transcriptional Activator]
    O --> K[Rob Transcriptional Regulator]
    F --> J[SoxR Sensor Protein]
    J --> Q

    P --> R{Is Efflux Gene Expression Upregulated?}
    Q --> R
    K --> R
    L[/AcrR Repressor Protein/] -- "Repression Lifted" --> R

    R -- "Yes" --> S[MarA Binds acrAB & other Efflux Promoters]
    R -- "Yes" --> T[SoxS Binds acrAB & other Efflux Promoters]
    R -- "Yes" --> U[Rob Binds acrAB & other Efflux Promoters]
    R -- "Yes" --> W[Chromosomal Efflux Gene Transcription]

    S --> X[mRNA Synthesis for Efflux Pumps]
    T --> X
    U --> X
    V --> X
    W --> X

    X --> Y[Ribosomal Translation of Efflux Pump mRNA]
    Y --> Z[Chaperone-mediated Protein Folding]
    Z --> AA[Insertion of Inner Membrane Components]
    AA --> AB[Periplasmic Protein Translocation]
    AB --> AC[Outer Membrane Protein Insertion]

    AC --> AD[RND Efflux Pump (e.g., AcrAB-TolC)]
    AC --> AE[MFS Efflux Pump (e.g., MdfA, TetA)]
    AC --> AF[SMR Efflux Pump (e.g., EmrE)]
    AC --> AG[MATE Efflux Pump (e.g., NorM)]
    AC --> AH[Plasmid-Encoded RND Pump (e.g., from Lv L paper)]

    AD --> AI[AcrA (Periplasmic Adaptor Protein)]
    AD --> AJ[AcrB (Inner Membrane Transporter)]
    AD --> AK[TolC (Outer Membrane Channel)]

    AI --> AL[AcrA-AcrB Complex Formation]
    AJ --> AL
    AL --> AM[TolC Docking with AcrA-AcrB]
    AK --> AM
    AM --> AN[Functional Tripartite Efflux Channel Formed]

    AN --> AT
    AE --> AT
    AF --> AT
    AG --> AT
    AH --> AT

    AT --> AW{Antibiotic Bound AND Energy Source Available?}
    AU[Proton Motive Force (PMF) Available] --> AW
    AV[ATP Available] --> AW

    AW -- "Yes" --> AX[Conformational Change of Efflux Pump]
    AX --> AY[Translocation of Antibiotic Across Membrane]
    AY --> AZ[Expulsion of Antibiotic from Cell]

    AZ --> BA[Reduced Intracellular Antibiotic Concentration]
    BA --> BB[/Antibiotic Below Minimum Inhibitory Concentration (MIC)/]
    BB --> BC[Increased Antibiotic Resistance]
    BC --> BD[/Multidrug Resistance Phenotype/]
    BD --> BE[Treatment Failure Potential]
    BE --> BF[Enhanced Bacterial Survival]

    AO[Tigecycline Molecules] --> AT
    AP[Fluoroquinolone Molecules] --> AT
    AQ[Tetracycline Molecules] --> AT
    AR[Beta-lactam Molecules (e.g., Sulopenem)] --> AT
    AS[Other Hydrophobic Cations & Amphiphiles] --> AT

sources.journal: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology
sources.title: A review of the mechanisms that confer antibiotic resistance in pathotypes of E. coli.
Plasmid-encoded tet(X) genes that confer high-level tigecycline resistance in Escherichia coli.
Emergence of a Plasmid-Encoded Resistance-Nodulation-Division Efflux Pump Conferring Resistance to Multiple Drugs, Including Tigecycline, in Klebsiella pneumoniae.
Sulopenem: An Intravenous and Oral Penem for the Treatment of Urinary Tract Infections Due to Multidrug-Resistant Bacteria.
